title: [RELAX] Fix rotary embedding buffer size calculation
body:
- Change head_dim//2 to rotary_dim//2 in LongRope scaling
- Fixes buffer size when rotary_dim differs from head_dim when partial_rotary_factor < 1.0

step_plan.summary: This plan introduces a new rotary scaling utility to fix the LongRope buffer sizing logic and integrates it into the Relax rotary embedding pathway, accompanied by unit tests to cover critical edge cases.

steps.operation: Add new rotary scaling utility module implementing corrected buffer size computation
steps.reason: Implement the fixed calculation: use rotary_dim//2 for the scaling and robust buffer-size computation when rotary_dim != head_dim and partial_rotary_factor < 1.0, matching PR intent.
steps.side_effects: Introduces a new API surface; downstream Relax modules must import and call this utility; may require updates to build/tests to recognize the new module

steps.operation: Refactor LongRope scaling to consume the new utility and apply corrected buffer configuration
steps.reason: Replace internal buffer-size calculation with the new.compute_buffer_size to ensure consistent behavior across head_dim/rotary_dim scenarios and partial_rotary_factor bounds.
steps.side_effects: Potential behavioral changes if old logic differed; necessitates updates to unit tests and any downstream callers relying on buffer sizing

steps.operation: Add unit tests validating the new buffer-size calculation and edge cases
steps.reason: Verify correctness when rotary_dim == head_dim, when rotary_dim != head_dim, and for partial_rotary_factor values including 0.0, 1.0, and <1.0; ensure no div-by-zero or negative sizes
steps.side_effects: Tests will increase coverage; may require minor test framework or fixture adjustments; ensures API reliability for future changes
